sections.paragraph:
παροργισμός, οῦ, ὁ (PsSol 8:9; LXX mostly act. ‘provoking to anger’: 3 Km 15:30; 4 Km 23:26 or ‘an action that calls forth anger’ in someone: 2 Esdr 19:18) state of being intensely provoked, angry mood, anger, pass. (Jer 21:5 w. θυμός and ὀργή) Eph 4:26 (cp. the Pythagorean saying Plut., Mor. 488bc; also 1QS 5:26–6:1; CD 9:6–8).—DELG s.v. ὀργή. M-M. TW.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

act. act. = active

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

1QS 1QS = The Manual of Discipline—List 2, end

CD CD = Cairo (Genizah text of the) Damascus (Document), a.k.a. Zadokite Document or Damascus Rule—List 2, end

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 780
